Notice Of Motion For Order To Apportion And Distribute Funds
Off Calendar Sep-17-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for hearing on the Motion for an Order to Apportion and Distribute Settlement Funds.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gerardo C. Sandoval. Clerk: K. Dougherty. Reporter: Giselle Casey,CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-551-3680. Email: gcasey@sftc.org.

Geoff Sloniker, Esq. of Brayton Purcell LLP appeared for plaintiffs.
Wrongful Death Heirs Karamella Myles and Sally Brooks were placed under oath and testified.
Both Ms. Myles and Ms. Brooks appeared via CourtCall for this morning's hearing.
No appearance by Wrongful Death Heirs David Brooks, Korey Brooks, William Brooks, Shaylen McAdams, Megan McAdams or Gavin McAdams.

The Court, having reviewed the moving papers and having considered the evidence, both oral and documentary, and good cause appearing, RULES that the following are entitled to distribution of all net funds obtained by their counsel, Brayton Purcell LLP, derived from all funds negotiated after the May 23, 2016 death of Russell Brooks in the following proportions:

Estate of Russell Brooks, Deceased - 0%
Karamella Myles, as Wrongful Death Heir - 25%
Sally Brooks, as Wrongful Death Heir - 25%
David Brooks, as Wrongful Death Heir - 0%
Korey Brooks, as Wrongful Death Heir - 25%
William Nathan Brooks, as Wrongful Death Heir - 25%
Shaylyn McAdams, as Wrongful Death Heir - 0%
Megan McAdams, as Wrongful Death Heir - 0%
Gavin McAdams, as Wrongful Death Heir - 0%

All future net funds derived from settlements entered into after the May 23, 2016 death of Russell Brooks shall be distributed in the percentages set forth above.

The signature of plaintiff Karamella Myles shall be sufficient on all settlement documents for the purpose of settling both the estate and wrongful death claims with defendants in all claims with settlements negotiated after Darryl Ford's death.

Counsel for plaintiffs shall prepare the proposed Order to Apportion and Distribute Funds and submit same to the Court for review and signature.
